items.title: 'Blessure Mbappé valt mee: Clásico mogelijk haalbaar'
items.summary: De blessure van Kylian Mbappé lijkt mee te vallen. Uit Franse berichtgeving blijkt dat zijn hamstringblessure niet van ernstige aard is. Een MRI-scan zal moeten uitwijzen hoelang de aanvaller van Real Madrid uit de roulatie is.

items.title: Everton 1-4 Chelsea in WSL, Coventry 3-1 Wrexham, Scottish Premiership – clockwatch
items.summary: ⚽ Keep up with all the latest football action ⚽ All Sunday’s fixtures | Mail Graham here Arsenal, the holders, take on Lyon in the Women’s Champions League. Suzanne Wrack has spoken to Olivia Smith: “As the season reaches its climax, a Champions League semi-final against Lyon on Sunday is testament to how far up the ladder Smith has climbed. Now, she is heading towards unknown territory: a second season at the same club for the first time in her senior career. “I do feel quite calm now, knowing that I have set down some roots here, but at the end of the day, football is football and you never know what’s next,” she says. “So I’m always on my toes but, right now, I’m kind of laid-back, just enjoying the time here in the present with Arsenal and looking forward to winning more silverware and growing as a player and a person.” I sorted out my mind: “Come on, Gigi,” I said to myself, and I gave myself strength: “When the game is over you can stop playing football. Just take on this hour and a half and then say goodbye to it all.” Continue reading...

items.title: Chelsea v Leeds United: FA Cup semi-final – live
items.summary: ⚽ Updates from the 3pm BST kick-off at Wembley ⚽ Live scores | Follow us on Bluesky Given Chelsea supporters are pining for the old days, perhaps they can cheer themselves up by remembering glorious runs from some of the club’s previous interim managers, although whether Calum McFarlane is capable of emulating the likes of Guus Hiddink, Roberto Di Matteo and Rafael Benítez looks like a long shot as another damaging week for the BlueCo project draws to a close. Is this inexperienced young coach the man for a salvage operation? Fans will take some convincing after watching Chelsea’s players not so much throw in the towel as not even bother to pick it up at all during Tuesday’s defeat to Brighton, which saw off Liam Rosenior. Those heading to Wembley for Sunday’s FA Cup semi-final against Leeds will hope for a response but do not be surprised if they turn mutinous again. Continue reading...

items.title: Arteta kraakt arbitrage: 'Als je hebt gevoetbald weet je dat dit rood is'
items.summary: Arsenal won zaterdag thuis nipt van Newcastle United en blijft daardoor meedoen om de titel in de Premier League. Mikel Arteta was echter niet te spreken over de arbitrage, want de Spanjaard vond dat doelman Nick Pope een rode kaart had moeten hebben. Ook tegen concurrent Manchester City voelde Arteta zich benadeeld door de arbitrage.

items.title: Zéér productieve Haps op punt van promotie naar Serie A met Venezia
items.summary: Venezia staat op het punt om terug te keren in de Serie A. De koploper van het tweede niveau van Italië kan volgend weekend promoveren. Ridgeciano Haps is de grote uitblinker in de afgelopen weken.

items.title: VI Live: doek kan vallen voor Heracles na ruime zege Excelsior
items.summary: Zondag staat er met twee duels een uitgedund Eredivisie-programma klaar. Excelsior neemt het op tegen FC Utrecht en FC Volendam gaat op bezoek bij Heracles Almelo. Gelukkig wordt er ook in het buitenland gevoetbald. Blijf op de hoogte met deze VI Live.

items.title: Vrouwen Ajax en Feyenoord schieten weinig op met remise in Klassieker
items.summary: In dit blog volgen we alle ontwikkelingen in het vrouwenvoetbal. We besteden onder meer aandacht aan de Eurojackpot Vrouwen Eredivisie, de Europese toernooien en het Nederlands elftal.

items.title: Why Rashford must be patient over Barcelona future
items.summary: Marcus Rashford is not ready to give up on his Barcelona dream just yet, writes Spanish football expert Guillem Balague.

items.title: Fifa agrees to increase World Cup prize money after countries raise concerns over costs
items.summary: Extra funding to be approved by Fifa Council this week National FAs feared losing money at this summer’s finals Fifa has agreed in principle to increase World Cup 2026 prize money and participation fees, with details of the enhanced funding to be approved at a meeting of the Fifa Council in Vancouver this week. World football’s governing body has responded to concerns raised by several national associations – first reported by The Guardian in February the high costs of travel, operations and tax in the United States in particular this summer will result in them losing money, even if their side has a successful tournament and reaches the latter stages. Continue reading...

items.title: A long time coming but something to build on - Isak's landmark goal
items.summary: Will Alexander Isak's first Liverpool Premier League goal at Anfield prove a turning point for the £125m signing?

items.title: Why being in an FA Cup semi-final means so much to Leeds fans
items.summary: BBC Sport looks at why the FA Cup semi-final against Chelsea means so much to long-suffering Leeds United fans.

items.title: LIVE: Tijd dringt voor Gent tegen Club Brugge!
items.summary: KAA Gent en Club Brugge willen in de Slag om Vlaanderen punten pakken. Ivan Leko doet dat met dezelfde elf namen die KV Mechelen zwaar klopten. Bij Gent is Lopes da Silva terug en verdwijnt Duverne zo uit de ploeg. Kanga en Dean staan voorin. Volg het hier LIVE en op de voet!

items.title: Nieuw enorm schandaal in Italië: scheidsrechtersbaas zet stap opzij na zware beschuldigingen
items.summary: Gianluca Rocchi wordt onderzocht voor sportfraude en heeft zichzelf geschorst na verdenkingen rond de arbitrage in Italië. Toch houdt hij vol dat hij onschuldig is.

items.title: 🎥 Nouveau clin d'oeil au Standard : Hugo Cuypers fait le show avec l'ancien chouchou de Ronny Deila
items.summary: Hugo Cuypers commence la saison de MLS en force. Cette nuit, il a encore trouvé le chemin des filets, contribuant à la très large victoire de son équipe.

items.title: Scheidsrechter Laforge had 24 minuten nodig om te bekomen: spanning liep hoog op in Westerlo tijdens rust
items.summary: Bij Westerlo bleef de frustratie na de nederlaag tegen Antwerp niet beperkt tot de rode kaart voor Emin Bayram. Ook wat er tijdens de rust gebeurde, dreigt nog een stevig staartje te krijgen.

items.title: Le paradoxe qui amuse le Nord : le probable retour de Thorgan Hazard à Lens fait parler
items.summary: Thorgan Hazard se dirige vers un retour à Lens. Une nouvelle qui fait bien rire du côté de Lille, où Eden a laissé sa trace.

items.title: Auteur du but victorieux en finale : un ancien espoir d'Anderlecht remporte le premier trophée de sa carrière
items.summary: Aaron Leya Iseka est aujourd'hui actif dans le championnat grec. Et il vient de s'y offrir le tout premier trophée de sa carrière.

items.title: Belgisch goudhaantje Mika Godts staat voor toptransfer: 'Europese topclubs schuiven aan'
items.summary: Mika Godts blijft week na week indruk maken bij Ajax en lijkt stilaan klaar voor een nog grotere stap. Na alweer een geweldige prestatie groeit het gevoel dat zijn transferzomer nu echt voor de deur staat.

items.title: Karel Geraerts moet vrezen bij Reims na harde uithaal van eigen aanhang
items.summary: Reims gaf gisterenavond de overwinning nog uit handen tegen Nancy. Karel Geraerts komt daardoor steeds meer onder druk te staan in de Champagne.

items.title: "Quand le chef des Ultras me passe le drapeau..." : Matthieu Epolo raconte son planté polémique au Mambourg
items.summary: Qui a dit que les Europe Playoffs étaient insipides et sans saveur en dehors de la première place ? Matthieu Epolo y a vécu quelques émotions.

items.title: "Adri en rit, mais moi ça me touche profondément" : la famille de Bertaccini choquée par les insultes en ligne
items.summary: Le duo Anderlecht-Bertaccini devait être une réussite. Pourtant, l'ancien buteur de Saint-Trond connaît des débuts compliqués à Bruxelles.

items.title: 'It's not done' - Arsenal back up Rice's words with crucial win
items.summary: Arsenal deliver on Declan Rice's promise that the Premier League title race is "not done" with an important win over Newcastle.

items.title: Mikel Arteta criticises red card escapes for Newcastle’s Pope and City’s Khusanov
items.summary: ‘It’s a red card today. It’s a red card in Manchester’ Arsenal return to top of table by beating Newcastle Mikel Arteta has claimed that everything would be different for his Arsenal team in the Premier League title race if Manchester City had been reduced to 10 men in last Sunday’s pivotal game at the Eithad Stadium. The Arsenal manager insisted that the City defender, Abdukodir Khusanov, should have been sent off for a last-man foul on Kai Havertz in the 53rd minute with the score at 1-1. City went on to win 2-1 and Arteta said nothing after the game about an incident that did not generate much controversy, the consensus being Khusanov had defended his position fairly. Continue reading...

items.title: Trophyless to treble? Man City's quest for more history
items.summary: Manchester City were in a state of turmoil this time last year – staring at a first trophyless season since 2017 – but they are now in with a chance of a domestic treble.

items.title: De Zerbi demands mentality shift at injury-hit Spurs: ‘We need to stay strong in the head’
items.summary: Solanke and Simons were taken off injured at Wolves Tottenham still in relegation zone because of West Ham win Robert De Zerbi is waiting on the fitness of two more players, after Dominic Solanke and Xavi Simons were forced off injured at Molineux on Saturday, but he is optimistic that Tottenham’s 1-0 victory at Wolves “can change something in our mentality”. The Spurs head coach celebrated his first win in charge of the relegation-threatened Europa League holders with the visiting fans in the Steve Bull Stand, exchanging fist pumps and belief. Continue reading...

items.title: FA Cup semi final: Joao Pedro starts, Palmer on bench for Chelsea vs Leeds LIVE!
Dolphins pick British tight end Traore in NFL Draft
items.summary: British tight end Seydou Traore is ready to take the NFL by storm after being selected by the Miami Dolphins with the 180th overall pick.

items.title: Wiesberger ends five-year wait for DP World Tour title
items.summary: Bernd Wiesberger ended his five-year wait for a DP World Tour title following victory at the China Open on Sunday.

items.title: Hong Kong superstar Ka Ying Rising extends winning streak to 20
items.summary: Ka Ying Rising cemented his status as the world’s best sprinter by extending his winning run to 20 with victory in the Chairman’s Sprint Prize at Sha Tin.

items.title: Record-breaker Sawe wins London Marathon in under two hours
items.summary: Sabastian Sawe of Kenya smashed the men's world record by winning the 2026 London Marathon in 1 hour, 59 minutes and 30 seconds on Sunday.
